title: DNS Failure
summary: Student troubleshoots a basic DNS name resolution failure using evidence-first diagnostics.
student_goal: Determine whether the issue is DNS resolution, network reachability, or incorrect client configuration.

required_evidence.label: IP connectivity test
required_evidence.description: Student must test whether the destination or gateway is reachable by IP.

required_evidence.label: DNS query result
required_evidence.description: Student must provide nslookup, dig, or equivalent DNS query output.

required_evidence.label: Client DNS configuration
required_evidence.description: Student must verify the configured DNS server on the client.

opening_questions: Can the client reach the destination by IP address?
What DNS server is the client using?
What result do you get from nslookup or dig?
mentor_guidance_rules: Do not assume DNS is the root cause until IP connectivity is tested.
Ask for query output before recommending configuration changes.
Separate DNS failure from routing or firewall failure.
